steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Before the blank: a complete independent clause ("The reed of a wind instrument is the mouthpiece"). The blank requires: a relative clause that modifies "mouthpiece."

steps.title: Apply the rule for word order in relative clauses
steps.content: Rule: a relative clause uses declarative word order (subject before verb), not question word order, and the full sentence ends with a period since it is a statement. Reconstructed sentence with each choice: A) "...the mouthpiece where is sound made." — inverted question word order in a declarative clause; grammatically incorrect; eliminated. B) "...the mouthpiece where sound is made?" — correct word order but ends with a question mark; the sentence is not a question; eliminated. C) "...the mouthpiece where sound is made." — declarative word order, ends with a period; grammatically correct. D) "...the mouthpiece where is sound made?" — inverted word order and wrong end punctuation; double error; eliminated. Verdict: C is correct.
